scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Global LNG glut deepens, JKM collapses toward $6, the FY2026 spread/optimization tailwind reverses, marginal recontracting prints sub-$2/MMBtu, and the market re-rates LNG from a premium growth name to a midstream/contracted-infra multiple (~8.5x).
scenarios.narrative_long: The largest supply wave in history (Qatar NFE/NFS + Plaquemines + Golden Pass + Port Arthur + CP2) lands into decelerating demand; JKM-HH compresses to shipping cost with episodic negative merchant margins. The 2026 raise unwinds as optimization/spread upside mean-reverts; Train 7/CCL4 and SPL Expansion FID into the trough at fees at/below legacy. The equity de-rates to ~8.5x EV/EBITDA - the midstream/contracted-infra analog - as the AI/LNG-demand premium deflates.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 8.5x P/E = midstream/contracted-infra band (KMI/EPD historical 8-11x EV/EBITDA-equivalent), below LNG's own forward ~12.3x. Reflects de-rating to a no-growth contracted-infra analog as the supply glut deflates the premium.

eps_bridge.label: FY2026 spread/optimization tailwind reverses + open-book margin normalizes into the glut
eps_bridge.source_tag: S1: LNG 10-K open-position sensitivity (<$50M per $1/MMBtu) + S3 JKM-to-$7 path

eps_bridge.label: Marginal recontracting / new-train fixed fee compresses toward ~$2/MMBtu (at/below legacy)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S3: Wolfe recontracting assumption; undisclosed marginal fee

eps_bridge.label: Higher rate / slower buyback at depressed FCF (lost per-share accretion)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S2: LNG capital-allocation framework (buyback pace scales with DCF)

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Soft glut: JKM eases toward $7-8, merchant margins normalize, recontracting is delayed rather than punitive, and the multiple compresses from ~10.2x toward ~9x (P/E ~10.5x) - the stock works back toward the sell-side floor / 52-week low.
scenarios.narrative_long: The supply wave pressures spreads but not catastrophically; the contracted book holds FY2027 EBITDA near $7.3B while the premium multiple erodes as base-rate energy-infra de-rating plays out. PT cuts begin (multiple cuts, estimates intact). The stock settles near the $200-220 sell-side floor / $186 52-week low.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 10.5x P/E ~ between the midstream floor and LNG's own forward ~12.3x; consistent with a partial de-rate of the AI/LNG premium with EBITDA intact.

eps_bridge.label: Open-book / spread normalization (JKM toward $7-8)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S3: JKM forward curve + LNG open-position sensitivity

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Contracted book holds, Stage 3 completes end-2026, buyback compounds DCF/share, FY2027 EBITDA lands near $7.8B; the stock holds roughly its current forward multiple (~13x P/E) as the contracted annuity + capital return offset the supply-wave overhang.
scenarios.narrative_long: The ~90%-contracted take-or-pay base delivers FY2027 Consolidated Adj EBITDA ~$7.8B; Stage 3 fully ramps and the >$10B buyback retires ~4-5%/yr of shares, so DCF/share grows even on a flat platform. The supply glut compresses merchant/portfolio margin but the fixed-fee core is insulated; the multiple holds ~13x (in line with the current ~12.3x FY27E forward) rather than re-rating up (capped by oversupply) or down (cushioned by the contracted annuity).
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 13.0x P/E ~ in line with LNG's own current forward (~12.3x FY27E); below VG/CQP-implied and the SRE/KMI utility-midstream band. Holds the multiple rather than re-rating either way.

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: New SPAs + SPL Expansion FID de-risk recontracting at premium fees, buyback accelerates on the raised ~$5B DCF, FY2027 EBITDA tops $8B; the market re-rates LNG toward ~15x as the contracted-growth-plus-capital-return story is recognized.
scenarios.narrative_long: Cheniere signs incremental 15yr+ SPAs at fees at/above legacy and reaches FID on SPL Expansion / Train 7, proving the growth pipeline clears even in oversupply. The raised DCF (~$5B) funds an accelerated buyback toward a ~$30/share run-rate, and the market credits the contracted-compounder narrative with a ~15x multiple (toward the Street's PT).
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 15.0x P/E = re-rate toward the Street PT (~$304.50 ~ 15.6x FY27E), reflecting recognition of the contracted-growth + capital-return compounding; above LNG's current forward but below utility multiples.

eps_bridge.label: Accelerated buyback compounding on raised ~$5B DCF (share count toward ~205M)
New FID accretion begins to price (SPL Expansion / Train 7-CCL4 optionality)

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: AI-power gas-demand supercycle: multiple new premium SPAs + FIDs convert the >100 mtpa permitted platform, JKM re-widens on demand, buyback drives DCF/share toward ~$30, and LNG re-rates to a CQP/utility-adjacent ~17x.
scenarios.narrative_long: Structural gas demand (Asia coal-to-gas + US/global data-center power) outruns even the record supply wave; Cheniere converts a large share of its permitted platform to premium long-dated SPAs and FIDs, the buyback shrinks the share count materially, and the market re-rates the contracted compounder toward ~17x - approaching the utility/contracted-infra premium band.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 17.0x P/E approaches a CQP/utility-adjacent premium; tail re-rate predicated on durable premium-fee recontracting + a demand supercycle absorbing the supply wave.

eps_bridge.label: Multiple incremental premium SPAs + FIDs convert the >100 mtpa permitted platform
eps_bridge.source_tag: S3: global LNG demand pipeline (AI-power gas, Asia coal-to-gas)

eps_bridge.label: Aggressive buyback toward ~$30/share run-rate DCF shrinks share count

headline.headline_text: Source-conditional Hold: LNG at $239.40 carries a de-biased 12-month expected return of +0.7%, conditional on the ~90%-contracted book holding its fixed fees and the FY2026 raise proving durable. Anchoring to independent fair value (~$244, ~flat) barely moves it - LNG's DCF was already near spot, so this confirms a genuine (not spot-anchored) Hold; scenario-weighted range -39.6% to +47.0%.
